# Workplan — Bullpen Omnibus Review
**Run date:** 2026-04-09
**OUTPUT_DIR:** docs/reviews/2026-04-09
**Status:** Read-only output document. Do not edit this file to track progress.
To track completion, update `docs/planning/todo.md` or use git commits.

---

## Prioritized Implementation Phases

Items are grouped into ~20-minute implementation phases, sequenced HIGH before MEDIUM before LOW. Within each phase, items are ordered by logical dependency.

---

### Phase 1 — HIGH Priority: Legal & License Foundation

**sec-add-login-rate-limit** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 01-security-audit.md — "HIGH — No Rate Limiting on Login Endpoint"
- Files: `requirements.txt`, `server/app.py`
- Changes: Add `flask-limiter` to `requirements.txt`. Import and configure `Limiter` in `server/app.py` with `key_func=get_remote_address`. Apply `@limiter.limit("5 per minute")` decorator to the `login_submit` route. Log failed attempts with IP and timestamp.
- Done when: `POST /login` with 6 requests from the same IP within 60 seconds returns HTTP 429 on the 6th request, confirmed by a test or manual curl sequence.
- Commit: `security: add rate limiting to login endpoint (5/min per IP)`

---

**legal-add-license-file** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 02-legal-compliance.md — "HIGH — No Software License"; 12-license-audit.md — "HIGH — No Project LICENSE File"
- Files: `LICENSE` (new file in repository root)
- Changes: Create `LICENSE` file at repository root. Choose MIT (consistent with all upstream dependencies) or proprietary notice per owner preference. MIT text: standard SPDX MIT template with year and author name.
- Done when: `ls LICENSE` in the repository root returns the file; the file contains a valid license text with author and year filled in.
- Commit: `legal: add MIT LICENSE file to repository root`

---

**ops-add-cicd-pipeline** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 06-tech-due-diligence.md — "HIGH — No CI/CD Pipeline"; 10-operational-practice.md — "HIGH — No CI/CD Pipeline"
- Files: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (new file)
- Changes: Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with a job that checks out the code, installs Python 3.11, runs `pip install -r requirements.txt`, and runs `pytest`. Add a second step running `ruff check server/ tests/` once ruff is configured (can be added in a follow-up).
- Done when: A push to any branch triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and `pytest` passes green in CI.
- Commit: `ops: add GitHub Actions CI pipeline (pytest on push/PR)`

---

**ops-add-structured-logging** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 10-operational-practice.md — "HIGH — No Structured Logging or Log Rotation"
- Files: `bullpen.py`, `server/app.py`
- Changes: In `bullpen.py`, replace any `print()` startup messages with `logging.info()`. Configure a `RotatingFileHandler` writing JSON-formatted lines to `.bullpen/logs/app.log` (max 10MB, 5 backups). Add `--log-level` CLI argument (choices: debug/info/warning/error, default: info). In `server/auth.py`, log every login attempt (success/failure) with IP and timestamp at INFO level.
- Done when: Starting Bullpen and performing a login attempt produces a structured JSON log entry in `.bullpen/logs/app.log` with timestamp, level, and event type fields.
- Commit: `ops: add structured rotating log handler and --log-level flag`

---

**deps-pin-requirements** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 06-tech-due-diligence.md — "HIGH — No Dependency Pinning or Lock File"
- Files: `requirements.txt`, `requirements-dev.txt` (new), `requirements.lock` (new, generated)
- Changes: Split `requirements.txt` into runtime deps (`flask`, `flask-socketio`, `eventlet`) and create `requirements-dev.txt` for dev deps (`pytest`, `pytest-cov`). Run `pip-compile requirements.txt -o requirements.lock` to generate a fully pinned lock file. Pin flask-socketio to an exact version. Update CI to install from `requirements.lock`.
- Done when: `pip install -r requirements.lock` produces a deterministic environment; `pip-compile --dry-run requirements.txt` exits 0 with no changes.
- Commit: `deps: pin all dependencies with pip-compile lock file`

---

### Phase 2 — HIGH Priority: Test & Scalability Infrastructure

**test-add-coverage-tooling** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 04-test-coverage.md — "HIGH — No Coverage Measurement Configured"
- Files: `requirements-dev.txt`, `pyproject.toml` (new or extend), `Makefile` (new or extend)
- Changes: Add `pytest-cov` to `requirements-dev.txt`. Add `[tool.coverage.run]` and `[tool.coverage.report]` sections to `pyproject.toml` (source = `server`, omit = `tests/*`). Add a `make coverage` target that runs `pytest --cov=server --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=75`. Add CI step to run coverage after tests.
- Done when: `make coverage` (or `pytest --cov=server --cov-report=term-missing`) exits 0 and prints a per-module coverage table for the `server/` package.
- Commit: `test: add pytest-cov coverage measurement with 75% threshold`

---

**scale-add-worker-output-log** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 09-scalability.md — "MEDIUM — Output Buffer Capped at 100KB with No Overflow to Disk"; 08-architecture.md — "MEDIUM — No Event Replay or Persistent Output Logs for Reconnection"
- Files: `server/workers.py`
- Changes: When a worker execution starts, open an append-only log file at `.bullpen/logs/<ws_id>-<slot_index>-<task_id>.log`. Write each output line to both the in-memory buffer (for real-time streaming) and this file (for durability). In the focus view reconnect path, serve historical output by reading this file rather than relying solely on the in-memory buffer.
- Done when: After a worker completes a task that produces >100KB of output, the full output is readable from the `.bullpen/logs/` file; reconnecting the focus view after a browser refresh shows complete output.
- Commit: `workers: add per-execution append-only output log for durability`

---

### Phase 3 — MEDIUM Priority: Security Hardening

**sec-scope-cors-origin** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 01-security-audit.md — "MEDIUM — CORS Wildcard When Binding to 0.0.0.0"
- Files: `server/app.py`
- Changes: Replace the `cors_origin = "*"` branch with a requirement that the operator pass an explicit `--public-url` flag when binding to `0.0.0.0`. If `--public-url` is absent and `host == "0.0.0.0"`, print a startup warning and set CORS to the machine's primary IP. Add `--cors-allow-all` flag as an explicit opt-in to wildcard CORS.
- Done when: Starting Bullpen with `--host 0.0.0.0` without `--public-url` prints a warning and does NOT set `cors_allowed_origins="*"`; `--cors-allow-all` still enables wildcard when explicitly requested.
- Commit: `security: require explicit --cors-allow-all instead of wildcard on 0.0.0.0`

---

**sec-fix-session-cookie-secure** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 01-security-audit.md — "MEDIUM — Session Cookie `Secure` Flag Always False"
- Files: `server/app.py`, `bullpen.py`
- Changes: Add a `--production` CLI flag to `bullpen.py`. When `--production` is set, configure `SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True` in the Flask app. Document in `docs/login.md` that `--production` should always be set when running behind an HTTPS reverse proxy.
- Done when: Starting with `--production` sets `app.config['SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE'] = True`; the session cookie sent by the login endpoint includes the `Secure` attribute in the response headers.
- Commit: `security: add --production flag to enable SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE`

---

**sec-harden-error-responses** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 01-security-audit.md — "LOW — Error Responses May Leak Internal Details"
- Files: `server/events.py`
- Changes: Audit all `except Exception as e` blocks in `server/events.py`. Replace `emit("error", {"message": str(e)})` in bare-exception handlers with `logger.exception(...)` server-side + `emit("error", {"message": "An internal error occurred."})` to the client. Implement the `handle_errors` decorator pattern described in the code quality review to standardize this across all event handlers.
- Done when: Triggering an unhandled exception in an event handler (e.g., by passing a task_id that causes a file-not-found error) emits the generic string to the client and logs the full traceback server-side; `str(e)` is not visible in the client-side error message.
- Commit: `security: sanitize unhandled exception messages in Socket.IO error responses`

---

**sec-fix-mcp-tempfile-cleanup** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 01-security-audit.md — "LOW — MCP Temp Config File Written to Disk"
- Files: `server/agents/claude_adapter.py`
- Changes: Wrap the MCP temp config file creation in a `try/finally` block (or use `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile` as a context manager) to guarantee cleanup on process crash or exception. Alternatively, use `NamedTemporaryFile(delete=True)` and pass the file handle to the subprocess via `/dev/fd/N`.
- Done when: If the agent subprocess is killed mid-execution, the temp config file is removed from disk within the cleanup path; verified by inspecting `/tmp` before and after a forced subprocess kill.
- Commit: `security: guarantee MCP temp config file cleanup on subprocess failure`

---

**legal-add-privacy-notice** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 02-legal-compliance.md — "MEDIUM — No Privacy Notice or Data Handling Policy"; 11-data-privacy-compliance.md — "HIGH — Task Data Sent to AI Providers Without In-Product Disclosure"
- Files: `docs/privacy.md` (new), `README.md`
- Changes: Create `docs/privacy.md` documenting: (a) all data stored locally (tasks, config, logs), (b) that no telemetry is collected, (c) that task content is sent to the configured AI provider and is subject to that provider's privacy policy, (d) links to Anthropic and OpenAI data processing terms. Add a one-sentence note in `README.md` linking to `docs/privacy.md`.
- Done when: `docs/privacy.md` exists and contains at minimum the four disclosure points above; `README.md` contains a link to it.
- Commit: `legal: add privacy notice documenting data storage and AI provider disclosure`

---

**legal-add-cla-dco** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 02-legal-compliance.md — "MEDIUM — No Contributor License Agreement"
- Files: `DCO.md` (new), `CONTRIBUTING.md` (new or extend)
- Changes: Create `DCO.md` with the Developer Certificate of Origin 1.1 text. Create or extend `CONTRIBUTING.md` to require `Signed-off-by:` in commit messages for external contributors. (For a proprietary project, a short CLA template should be substituted.)
- Done when: `DCO.md` exists at the repository root; `CONTRIBUTING.md` references the sign-off requirement.
- Commit: `legal: add DCO and contribution guidelines`

---

**brand-add-copyright-headers** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 03-brand-ip-audit.md — "MEDIUM — No Copyright Notices in Source Files"
- Files: `server/*.py`, `server/agents/*.py`, `bullpen.py`, `static/app.js`, `static/utils.js`, `static/components/*.js`
- Changes: Add a standard copyright header comment to each source file: `# Copyright (c) 2025-2026 [Author]. All rights reserved.` (Python) or equivalent JS comment block. This is a single-pass mechanical change.
- Done when: All `.py` files in `server/` and `bullpen.py` contain a copyright comment on the first or second line; all `.js` files in `static/` contain an equivalent comment.
- Commit: `legal: add copyright notice headers to all source files`

---

**lic-add-notice-file** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 12-license-audit.md — "MEDIUM — No NOTICE / THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES File"
- Files: `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` (new)
- Changes: Create `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` listing each third-party component (Flask, Werkzeug, Flask-SocketIO, eventlet, Vue.js 3, Socket.IO Client, markdown-it, Prism.js), its version, license type, and the upstream copyright notice text. Include CDN-loaded libraries.
- Done when: `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md` exists and contains entries for all 8 identified third-party components with license type and copyright holder.
- Commit: `legal: add THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES file for all upstream components`

---

**lic-pin-vue-sri** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 12-license-audit.md — "MEDIUM — Vue.js CDN Not Version-Pinned, No SRI Hash"
- Files: `static/index.html`
- Changes: Replace `https://unpkg.com/vue@3/dist/vue.global.prod.js` with a pinned version URL (e.g., `https://unpkg.com/vue@3.5.13/dist/vue.global.prod.js`) and add a corresponding `integrity="sha384-..."` SRI attribute. Verify the SRI hash against the downloaded file.
- Done when: The Vue script tag in `static/index.html` has both a pinned version in the URL and a valid `integrity` attribute; loading the page with an incorrect SRI hash causes the browser to block the script.
- Commit: `security: pin Vue.js CDN to specific version with SRI hash`

---

**qual-add-type-annotations** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 05-code-quality.md — "MEDIUM — No Type Annotations Anywhere in the Backend"
- Files: `server/validation.py`, `server/persistence.py`, `server/tasks.py`, `server/workers.py`
- Changes: Add type annotations to all public function signatures in `server/validation.py` and `server/persistence.py` (highest priority: stable interfaces). Add a `py.typed` marker file to `server/`. Configure `mypy` in `pyproject.toml` with `strict = false` and `ignore_missing_imports = true` as a starting baseline.
- Done when: `mypy server/validation.py server/persistence.py` exits 0 with no type errors reported; a `py.typed` marker exists in `server/`.
- Commit: `quality: add type annotations to validation and persistence modules`

---

**qual-fix-global-state** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 05-code-quality.md — "MEDIUM — Global Mutable State in Auth and Workers Modules"
- Files: `server/workers.py`, `server/workspace_manager.py`
- Changes: Move `_processes` dict from module-level global in `server/workers.py` into the `WorkspaceState` object in `server/workspace_manager.py` as an instance attribute. Update all call sites that reference `workers._processes` to go through the `WorkspaceState` instance. This eliminates cross-workspace state leakage and scopes process lifetime to workspace lifetime.
- Done when: `server/workers.py` has no module-level mutable dict for process tracking; all worker process state is accessed via `workspace_state.processes`; all 198 tests pass.
- Commit: `refactor: move worker process state from module global into WorkspaceState`

---

**arch-workspace-write-locks** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 08-architecture.md — "MEDIUM — Write Lock Is a Single Global Mutex"; 09-scalability.md — "MEDIUM — Write Lock Serializes All Workspace Mutations"
- Files: `server/locks.py`, `server/workspace_manager.py`, `server/events.py`
- Changes: Move `write_lock` from the module-level global in `server/locks.py` into `WorkspaceState` as an instance attribute. Update `server/events.py` to acquire `current_workspace.write_lock` rather than the global lock. Remove or repurpose `server/locks.py` once the global lock is removed.
- Done when: Two concurrent Socket.IO events targeting different workspaces do not block each other on the write lock; all 198 tests pass.
- Commit: `refactor: move write lock into WorkspaceState for per-workspace isolation`

---

**arch-scheduler-via-queue** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 08-architecture.md — "MEDIUM — Scheduler Bypasses Task Queue, Triggers May Be Silently Dropped"
- Files: `server/scheduler.py`, `server/workers.py`
- Changes: Replace the direct `start_worker()` call in `server/scheduler.py` trigger handlers with a call to `assign_task()` (or the equivalent task-queuing function), so scheduled triggers are enqueued rather than immediately executed. Add guard logic so a trigger is skipped (and logged) if the worker already has a queued task from a prior trigger.
- Done when: When a scheduled worker fires while already executing a task, the new trigger is enqueued rather than silently dropped; a test confirms the queue depth increases by 1 for a busy worker receiving a scheduled trigger.
- Commit: `scheduler: route trigger activations through task queue instead of direct start`

---

**test-add-scheduler-tests** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 04-test-coverage.md — "MEDIUM — Scheduler Module Lightly Tested"
- Files: `tests/test_scheduler.py`
- Changes: Expand `tests/test_scheduler.py` using `monkeypatch` on `datetime.datetime.now()` or `time.time()`. Add tests for: (a) `at_time` trigger fires exactly once per day, (b) `on_interval` trigger respects elapsed time and does not fire early, (c) a worker already running suppresses a redundant trigger, (d) lock contention does not deadlock under concurrent trigger checks.
- Done when: `pytest tests/test_scheduler.py -v` passes all 4+ new test cases with no sleeps or real-time waits; tests complete in under 2 seconds.
- Commit: `test: expand scheduler tests with monkeypatched time and edge case coverage`

---

**test-add-cors-tests** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 04-test-coverage.md — "MEDIUM — No CORS or Network-Level Security Tests"
- Files: `tests/test_cors.py` (new)
- Changes: Create `tests/test_cors.py` with Flask test client cases covering: (a) default host `127.0.0.1` → CORS `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header matches `http://127.0.0.1:<port>`, (b) `host=0.0.0.0` → CORS is `*` only when `--cors-allow-all` is set, (c) preflight OPTIONS request to a scoped-CORS server from a different origin returns 403/blocked.
- Done when: `pytest tests/test_cors.py -v` passes all 3 cases; these tests would have caught the CORS wildcard regression described in the security audit.
- Commit: `test: add CORS policy tests for default and 0.0.0.0 host bindings`

---

**test-add-worker-integration-tests** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 04-test-coverage.md — "MEDIUM — Worker Execution Tests Use Mock Adapter Only"
- Files: `tests/test_worker_integration.py` (new), `tests/fixtures/mock_agent.py` (new)
- Changes: Create a simple Python script `tests/fixtures/mock_agent.py` that prints lines of output and exits cleanly. Create `tests/test_worker_integration.py` that configures a worker with this script as its agent, assigns a task, waits for completion, and asserts: (a) output lines were streamed, (b) task status is `done`, (c) worker status returns to `idle`, (d) output log file exists in `.bullpen/logs/`.
- Done when: `pytest tests/test_worker_integration.py -v` passes using the real subprocess path (not MockAdapter); the test covers streaming, completion, and log file creation.
- Commit: `test: add worker integration tests using real subprocess execution path`

---

**ops-add-health-endpoint** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 10-operational-practice.md — "MEDIUM — No Health Check Endpoint"
- Files: `server/app.py`, `bullpen.py`
- Changes: Add `GET /health` route to `server/app.py` that returns `{"status": "ok", "version": __version__}` with no authentication required. Add `__version__ = "0.1.0"` constant to `bullpen.py`. Import and use it in the health endpoint.
- Done when: `curl http://localhost:<port>/health` (without auth cookie) returns `{"status": "ok", "version": "0.1.0"}` with HTTP 200.
- Commit: `ops: add /health endpoint and application version constant`

---

**ops-add-backup-docs** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 10-operational-practice.md — "MEDIUM — No Backup Mechanism for Task Data"
- Files: `docs/backup.md` (new), `bullpen.py`
- Changes: Create `docs/backup.md` documenting the recommended backup strategy (include `.bullpen/` in git, or use Time Machine/rsync). Add `bullpen --export-workspace PATH` CLI command that archives `.bullpen/` to a zip file using `shutil.make_archive`.
- Done when: `python3 bullpen.py --export-workspace /tmp/backup.zip` creates a zip file containing the `.bullpen/` directory; `docs/backup.md` exists and documents the restore procedure.
- Commit: `ops: add --export-workspace command and backup documentation`

---

**ops-add-deployment-docs** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 10-operational-practice.md — "MEDIUM — Process Lifecycle Not Documented for Production"
- Files: `docs/deployment.md` (new)
- Changes: Create `docs/deployment.md` documenting: (a) running Bullpen as a systemd service (include a working unit file template), (b) nginx reverse proxy configuration with TLS termination, (c) required environment variables, (d) upgrade procedure (stop service, git pull, restart).
- Done when: `docs/deployment.md` exists with systemd unit template and nginx config example; a developer can follow the docs to deploy on a clean Ubuntu 22.04 server without prior Bullpen knowledge.
- Commit: `docs: add production deployment guide (systemd, nginx, TLS)`

---

**priv-add-ai-disclosure** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 11-data-privacy-compliance.md — "HIGH — Task Data Sent to AI Providers Without In-Product Disclosure"
- Files: `static/components/WorkerConfigModal.js`, `README.md`
- Changes: Add a visible disclaimer in the worker configuration modal: "Task content is sent to [configured AI provider] for processing. Review [provider]'s privacy policy before including sensitive data in tasks." Update `README.md` with a data handling section referencing `docs/privacy.md`.
- Done when: Opening the worker configuration modal in the UI shows the AI provider disclosure text; `README.md` contains the data handling section.
- Commit: `privacy: add in-product AI provider data disclosure to worker config modal`

---

**priv-add-workspace-delete** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 11-data-privacy-compliance.md — "MEDIUM — No Data Deletion or Export Tool"
- Files: `bullpen.py`
- Changes: Add `bullpen --delete-workspace WORKSPACE_PATH` CLI command that removes the `.bullpen/` directory from the specified workspace path after a confirmation prompt. Combine with the `--export-workspace` command (ops-add-backup-docs) as a paired delete/export workflow.
- Done when: `python3 bullpen.py --delete-workspace /path/to/workspace` prompts for confirmation and, on "yes", removes `.bullpen/` from that path; the workspace is no longer listed in `bullpen --list-workspaces`.
- Commit: `privacy: add --delete-workspace command for workspace data erasure`

---

**qual-add-linter** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 05-code-quality.md — "LOW — No Linter or Formatter Configuration"
- Files: `pyproject.toml`, `requirements-dev.txt`
- Changes: Add `ruff` to `requirements-dev.txt`. Add `[tool.ruff]` section to `pyproject.toml` with `line-length = 100`, `select = ["E", "F", "I"]` (errors, pyflakes, isort). Add a CI step that runs `ruff check server/ tests/`. Run `ruff --fix` once to auto-fix any existing issues.
- Done when: `ruff check server/ tests/` exits 0 with no violations; CI fails if a future commit introduces a linting error.
- Commit: `quality: add ruff linter configuration`

---

### Phase 4 — MEDIUM Priority: Accessibility & Architecture

**a11y-semantic-buttons** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 07-accessibility.md — "HIGH — Interactive Divs Without Keyboard Access"
- Files: `static/components/WorkerCard.js`, `static/components/TaskCard.js`, `static/components/TopToolbar.js`, `static/components/KanbanTab.js`, `static/components/BullpenTab.js`
- Changes: Replace `<div @click="...">` interactive elements with `<button @click="...">` for all action controls (start, stop, configure, delete worker; task card selection; toolbar icon buttons). Remove `tabindex="0"` where added to divs — `<button>` is natively focusable. Ensure existing `class="btn"` styling applies to `<button>` elements.
- Done when: All action controls in the main application are reachable and activatable via keyboard Tab + Enter/Space; verified with VoiceOver or axe browser extension showing no "interactive element is not keyboard-accessible" violations.
- Commit: `a11y: replace interactive div elements with semantic button elements`

---

**a11y-aria-labels** · Priority: HIGH
Source: 07-accessibility.md — "HIGH — No ARIA Labels on Action Controls"
- Files: `static/components/WorkerCard.js`, `static/components/TopToolbar.js`, `static/components/BullpenTab.js`, `static/components/KanbanTab.js`
- Changes: Add `aria-label="<action>"` to all icon-only buttons. For buttons with visible text labels, use `aria-labelledby` referencing the text element. Examples: `aria-label="Start worker"`, `aria-label="Configure worker"`, `aria-label="Delete worker"`, `aria-label="Toggle theme"`.
- Done when: Running axe accessibility checker on the main application shows zero "Buttons must have an accessible name" violations; VoiceOver announces the button purpose when focused.
- Commit: `a11y: add aria-label to all icon-only action controls`

---

**a11y-landmark-regions** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 07-accessibility.md — "MEDIUM — No Landmark Regions in Main Application"
- Files: `static/app.js`, `static/index.html`, `static/components/LeftPane.js`
- Changes: Wrap the left task-list pane in `<aside aria-label="Task list">`. Wrap the main tab area in `<main id="main-content">`. Wrap the top toolbar in `<header>` or `<nav aria-label="Primary navigation">`. Add a visually-hidden skip link `<a href="#main-content" class="skip-link">Skip to main content</a>` as the first element in `<body>`.
- Done when: axe checker reports zero "Page must contain a level-one heading" or "All page content must be contained by landmarks" violations; the skip link is visible on first Tab press.
- Commit: `a11y: add semantic landmark regions and skip navigation link`

---

**a11y-modal-focus-trap** · Priority: MEDIUM
Source: 07-accessibility.md — "MEDIUM — Focus Management in Modals"
- Files: `static/components/TaskCreateModal.js`, `static/components/WorkerConfigModal.js`
- Changes: On modal open, call `nextTick(() => modalRef.value.querySelector('input, button')?.focus())` to move focus to the first interactive element. Store a reference to the triggering element before opening. On modal close, call `triggerEl.focus()` to return focus to the trigger. Add keyboard trap: Tab within modal should cycle only through modal focusables (prevent focus escaping to background).
- Done when: Opening a modal via keyboard moves focus into the modal; Tab cycles within the modal without escaping; closing the modal (Escape or close button) returns focus to the triggering element.
- Commit: `a11y: add focus trap and focus restoration to modal dialogs`

---

**arch-workspace-registry-lock** · Priority: LOW
Source: 08-architecture.md — "LOW — Workspace Registry Is Not Transactional Under Concurrent Writes"
- Files: `server/workspace_manager.py`
- Changes: Wrap `register_project()` and `remove_project()` in a `threading.Lock` that is held for the full read-modify-write cycle of `projects.json`. Alternatively, acquire the existing workspace write lock before modifying the registry.
- Done when: Concurrent calls to `register_project()` from two threads do not produce a corrupted or incomplete `projects.json`; verified by a stress test that calls `register_project()` 100 times concurrently and checks that all 100 entries appear in the registry.
- Commit: `fix: serialize workspace registry read-modify-write under a lock`

---

### Phase 5 — LOW Priority: Quality, Operations, Observability

**qual-error-handler-decorator** · Priority: LOW
Source: 05-code-quality.md — "LOW — Inconsistent Error Handling Patterns"
- Files: `server/events.py`
- Changes: Implement the `handle_errors(event_name)` decorator described in the code quality review. Apply it to all Socket.IO event handler functions in `server/events.py`. Remove duplicated try/except blocks that are replaced by the decorator.
- Done when: All event handlers in `server/events.py` use the `@handle_errors` decorator; there are no bare `except Exception as e: emit(str(e))` blocks remaining; unhandled exceptions log to the server and emit the generic string to the client.
- Commit: `quality: add handle_errors decorator for consistent Socket.IO error handling`

---

**qual-add-structured-logging** · Priority: LOW
Source: 05-code-quality.md — "LOW — No Structured Logging"
- Files: `bullpen.py`, `server/app.py`
- Changes: Add `python-json-logger` to `requirements.txt`. Configure Python's logging with a `JsonFormatter` at startup in `bullpen.py`. Replace any remaining `print()` calls in server modules with `logger.info()/debug()/error()` calls.
- Done when: All application log output (startup, auth, errors) is JSON-formatted; no `print()` calls remain in `server/*.py`; `grep -r "print(" server/` returns no matches.
- Commit: `quality: switch to JSON-structured logging across all server modules`

---

**ops-add-version** · Priority: LOW
Source: 10-operational-practice.md — "LOW — No Version Number in the Application"
- Files: `bullpen.py`
- Changes: Add `__version__ = "0.1.0"` constant to `bullpen.py`. Add `--version` flag to the CLI argument parser that prints the version and exits. (This overlaps with ops-add-health-endpoint which also uses this constant.)
- Done when: `python3 bullpen.py --version` prints `bullpen 0.1.0` and exits 0.
- Commit: `ops: add --version flag and __version__ constant`

---

**ops-add-vuln-scanning** · Priority: LOW
Source: 10-operational-practice.md — "LOW — No Dependency Vulnerability Scanning"
- Files: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- Changes: Add a `pip-audit` step to the CI pipeline that runs after `pip install -r requirements.lock` and fails if any known CVE is detected in the installed packages. Enable GitHub Dependabot alerts in the repository settings (documented in `docs/deployment.md`).
- Done when: CI runs `pip-audit` and the step is visible in the Actions log; a known-vulnerable package version in `requirements.lock` causes the CI step to fail.
- Commit: `ops: add pip-audit vulnerability scanning to CI pipeline`

---

**lic-remove-eventlet** · Priority: LOW
Source: 12-license-audit.md — "MEDIUM — eventlet Deprecated; Maintenance Risk"
- Files: `requirements.txt`, `server/app.py`
- Changes: Verify the current `async_mode` setting in `server/app.py`. If it is `"threading"` (not `"eventlet"`), remove `eventlet` from `requirements.txt` entirely. If `async_mode` relies on eventlet, evaluate replacing it with `gevent` or pure threading mode. Run the full test suite to confirm no regressions.
- Done when: `import eventlet` is not required at any point during `python3 bullpen.py` startup; all 198 tests pass; `pip install -r requirements.txt` does not install eventlet.
- Commit: `deps: remove deprecated eventlet dependency`

---

**lic-add-license-scanning** · Priority: LOW
Source: 12-license-audit.md — "LOW — No Automated License Scanning in CI"
- Files: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `requirements-dev.txt`
- Changes: Add `pip-licenses` to `requirements-dev.txt`. Add a CI step that runs `pip-licenses --fail-on="GPL;AGPL;LGPL"` after installing dependencies. This fails the build if any copyleft dependency is introduced.
- Done when: CI runs `pip-licenses` and the check is visible in the Actions log; adding a hypothetical GPL package to requirements would cause this step to fail.
- Commit: `ops: add pip-licenses copyleft check to CI pipeline`

---

**priv-add-audit-log** · Priority: LOW
Source: 11-data-privacy-compliance.md — "LOW — No Audit Log for Data Access"
- Files: `server/events.py`, `server/app.py`
- Changes: Add a dedicated audit logger in `bullpen.py` writing to `.bullpen/logs/audit.log` in JSON-lines format. Log: file access events from the Files tab (path, action, timestamp), task mutations (task_id, action, timestamp), and agent execution starts/completions (worker_id, task_id, timestamp). Do not log task content — log only metadata.
- Done when: After performing file browse, task create, and worker start operations, `.bullpen/logs/audit.log` contains corresponding JSON entries with timestamp, event_type, and resource_id fields.
- Commit: `privacy: add structured audit log for file access and task mutation events`

---

**scale-single-scheduler** · Priority: LOW
Source: 09-scalability.md — "LOW — Scheduler Thread Per Workspace"
- Files: `server/scheduler.py`, `server/workspace_manager.py`
- Changes: Replace the per-workspace `Scheduler` daemon thread with a single shared scheduler that maintains a priority queue keyed by next trigger time. The shared scheduler dispatches triggers to the appropriate workspace. Remove per-workspace `Scheduler` instances from `WorkspaceState`.
- Done when: With 5 registered workspaces, only 1 scheduler thread is created (verifiable via `threading.enumerate()` in tests); all scheduler trigger tests pass.
- Commit: `scheduler: replace per-workspace threads with a single shared scheduler`

---

**scale-task-index** · Priority: LOW
Source: 09-scalability.md — "LOW — Task File Iteration Is O(n) Per Read"
- Files: `server/tasks.py`, `server/persistence.py`
- Changes: Add a `tasks/index.json` file in each `.bullpen/` workspace that caches task metadata (id, title, status, priority, tags, order_key) per task. Update `tasks.py` to read from the index for list operations and update the index on create/update/delete. Fall back to full file scan if the index is absent (for backward compatibility).
- Done when: Listing 1000 tasks reads from `index.json` rather than parsing 1000 `.md` files; verified by adding timing assertions in a test that the index read is >10x faster than the full scan.
- Commit: `perf: add task metadata index file to reduce list-tasks from O(n) to O(1)`

---

**test-add-mutation-testing** · Priority: LOW
Source: 04-test-coverage.md — "LOW — No Mutation Testing"
- Files: `pyproject.toml`, `requirements-dev.txt`
- Changes: Add `mutmut` to `requirements-dev.txt`. Add a `make mutation-test` target that runs `mutmut run --paths-to-mutate server/validation.py,server/auth.py`. Run once and document the mutation score in the test coverage section of `docs/`.
- Done when: `mutmut run` completes against `server/validation.py` and produces a mutation score report; the score is documented in `docs/test-coverage.md` or equivalent.
- Commit: `test: add mutmut mutation testing for validation and auth modules`

---

## Additional Reviews Recommended for Next Cycle

- **Performance profiling review:** Instrument a representative workload (20 workers, 500 tasks, 3 workspaces) and profile the write lock hold time, scheduler wakeup latency, and Socket.IO event throughput to establish a baseline before the scaling work begins.
- **MCP integration deep-dive:** The `server/mcp_tools.py` module (455 lines) and the MCP stdio protocol implementation were not reviewed in depth. A dedicated review focused on: tool call injection risk, subprocess privilege escalation, and MCP spec compliance is warranted.
- **Frontend security review:** A targeted review of `static/app.js` and `static/components/*.js` for XSS vectors in markdown rendering (markdown-it output injection), template injection in Vue 3, and unsafe `innerHTML` usage.
- **Agent adapter security review:** A deep-dive into `server/agents/claude_adapter.py` and `server/agents/codex_adapter.py` focusing on prompt injection via task content, shell metacharacter handling in subprocess args, and the security implications of `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.

---

## Session Restart Prompt

To resume working through this workplan in a new session, copy the following prompt:

> I am working through the Bullpen codebase improvements identified in the review cycle at `docs/reviews/2026-04-09/`. Read `workplan.md` in that directory. Check `docs/planning/todo.md` or recent git commits to determine which phases are complete. Continue with the next incomplete phase. Do not re-run the review generation — only implement workplan items.
